title: Banana Oat Pancakes

subs.name: 1 tbsp flax seeds soaked in 3 tbsp water for 5 minutes (flax egg)
subs.notes: skip egg for a vegan option; pancakes will be slightly denser

subs.name: rice flour or ragi (finger millet) flour for a gluten-free or healthier version
subs.notes: increase milk by 2 tbsp if batter feels too thick

subs.name: coconut milk or any plant-based milk for a dairy-free version

steps.text: Mash the bananas in a large bowl with a fork until only small lumps remain. The riper the bananas — ideally very spotty — the sweeter and more fragrant the pancakes will be.

Add the eggs, milk and vanilla to the mashed banana. Whisk together until smooth. Fold in the wheat flour, oats and baking powder. Stir briefly until just combined — a few lumps are fine and preferable to over-mixing, which makes the pancakes tough. Rest the batter for 3 minutes.

Heat a tawa or flat frying pan on medium flame. Add a tiny amount of butter and swirl to coat. When the butter just begins to foam, pour in roughly 3 tbsp of batter per pancake — leave space between each. Cook until bubbles appear on the surface and the edges look set, about 2–2.5 minutes.

Flip each pancake with a flat spatula and cook the second side for 1.5–2 minutes until golden. The pancake should spring back when gently pressed. Repeat for the remaining batter, adding a little butter before each batch.
Stack the finished pancakes on a warm plate. Drizzle with honey and serve immediately — or top with sliced banana for double banana impact.

cross_contact_notes: Ingredient metadata cannot prove an allergen-free kitchen. Check packaged labels and cross-contact statements for your products.
critical_checks: Use pasteurised eggs for preparations served raw or lightly cooked, especially for higher-risk people.
trust.provenance_summary: AI-assisted original draft; declared licence original.
trust.substitution_warning: A substitution can introduce allergens or change dietary suitability. Never claim a swap is safe without checking the replacement's label and the user's allergy context.

substitution_notes: wheat-flour → rice flour or ragi (finger millet) flour for a gluten-free or healthier version (increase milk by 2 tbsp if batter feels too thick); milk → coconut milk or any plant-based milk for a dairy-free version; egg → 1 tbsp flax seeds soaked in 3 tbsp water for 5 minutes (flax egg) (skip egg for a vegan option; pancakes will be slightly denser); honey → jaggery syrup or sugar
indian_kitchen_adaptation: Ripe desi bananas (Mysore/Ney Poovan/Robusta) work beautifully here — they mash smoother and are sweeter than supermarket Cavendish. Use whole-wheat atta, which gives a slightly nuttier result than maida. Cook on a cast-iron tawa on medium-low gas flame; Indian gas flames can be very hot, so reduce to avoid burning the bottom before the inside sets. Jaggery syrup (melt vellam with a splash of water) makes an excellent local topping in place of honey.
